title: Be Civic — Customer-side profile (v1)
description: Validates the v1 customer-side profile.json per privacy.md §8.7.4 (and schemas.md §6.1.x). All date fields use YYYY-MM month-bucket precision. No identity-bearing fields are permitted; additionalProperties: false enforces the closed set. Migration notes (2026-05-18 fold of universal onboarding-rebuild proposal): (1) document_inventory.has_eID (boolean) is renamed to has_id_card (enum yes/not-yet-waiting/no/not-sure); legacy true → 'yes', legacy false → 'no'. (2) other_languages list is superseded by conversation_language (free-text up to 32 chars); other_languages[0] migrates to conversation_language on first write. (3) New browser_driving_preference field (enum drive-by-default/ask-each-time/never-drive) honoured at path-traversal time. (4) New typed consent: object namespace as an extensibility hook; specific keys (e.g. alpha_bundle/signed_at/version) are operational and vary by phase — consumers MUST tolerate unknown consent.* keys.

profile_schema_version.description: Schema version sentinel. Written on first create and on schema upgrade. Always 1 for v1 conforming profiles.

oneOf.description: ISO 8601 timestamp written on every write. Used for staleness detection.

last_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last write, or null when the profile has never been written.

region.description: Belgian administrative region of the customer's commune of residence.

oneOf.description: NIS5 commune code (5-digit string). No commune name, no address.
Language of the commune's administration, constrained to the commune's official languages.

Free-text language the user wants the agent to communicate in. Any language the agent can speak works (English, French, Tagalog, Slovenian, etc.) — not enum-constrained per D27. Distinct from administration_language (the commune's official language used for filings). Replaces the prior other_languages ordered list (D27 / 2026-05-18 fold); legacy other_languages[0] is migrated here on first write under V1 schema. Agents MUST tolerate legacy other_languages on read but MUST NOT write that field under V1.

User preference for browser-driving handoffs at path-traversal time. drive-by-default = agent drives the browser without confirmation when handoff is needed; ask-each-time = agent asks before each handoff; never-drive = agent emits markdown links and lets the user navigate themselves. Honoured per architecture.md §24.9 (Chrome MCP handoff vs AskUserQuestion vs markdown-link). New field per D8 (2026-05-18 fold).

consent.description: Typed namespace for consent metadata. The schema declares the namespace; specific keys are operational and vary by phase. Alpha-phase keys (e.g. alpha_bundle, signed_at, version) are documented in cowork-plugin.md §3.8 and are NOT locked here. Post-alpha keys for granular per-stream opt-out will be added when that posture lands. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown consent.* keys — the namespace is intentionally permissive (additionalProperties: true). New namespace per D9/D49 (2026-05-18 fold).

civic_status.description: Civil status as recognised by Belgian law.

nationality_status.description: Broad nationality category. 'BE' = Belgian only; 'EU' = EU/EEA non-Belgian; 'non-EU' = third-country national; 'multiple' = holds nationalities from more than one of the above groups.

residency_status.description: Current registration status in the Belgian population register.

start.description: Start of the residency period, YYYY-MM month-bucket precision.

end.description: End of the residency period (YYYY-MM), or null when the period is ongoing.

visa_type.description: Free-form visa type label (e.g. 'D-visa', 'short-stay C'). No document number.

permit_type.description: Residence permit type (e.g. 'F card', 'B card', 'EU blue card'). No document number.

residency_history.description: Chronological list of residency periods. Periods only — no document numbers.

dependents.description: Counts and booleans only; no names.

start.description: Start of the employment period, YYYY-MM month-bucket precision.

end.description: End of the employment period (YYYY-MM), or null when ongoing.

type.description: Employment type. FT = full-time employee; PT = part-time employee.

days_per_week.description: Contracted working days per week, or null when not applicable.

total_days_estimate.description: Estimated total working days in this period, for use in article 12bis economic-participation calculations. No employer names, no ONSS numbers.

employment_history.description: Chronological list of employment periods. No employer names, no ONSS numbers.

start.description: Start of the education period, YYYY-MM month-bucket precision.

end.description: End of the education period (YYYY-MM), or null when ongoing.

level.description: Education level label (e.g. 'secondary', 'higher-education', 'A2-language-certificate'). No institution name, no diploma number.

country_of_institution.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 lowercase country code of the institution.

education_history.description: Chronological list of education periods. No institution names, no diploma numbers.

oneOf.description: Whether the customer holds a Belgian-issued chip card (eID or residence card). Renamed from has_eID (D23, 2026-05-18 fold) — the prior eID-vs-residence-card distinction is dropped because all Belgian-issued chip cards are functionally equivalent for itsme/identity purposes; card-type-specific path-source eligibility is disambiguated at path-traversal time (D52), not at onboarding. Migration: legacy has_eID true → 'yes', has_eID false → 'no'. New enum values ('not-yet-waiting', 'not-sure') originate only from V1+ onboarding forms.

has_residence_card.description: Whether the customer holds a Belgian residence permit card (A through M series). Retained alongside has_id_card for documentary completeness; the chip-card-equivalence rule means has_id_card is the routing field, while has_residence_card is informational about which specific card type the holder carries.

has_NN.description: Whether the customer has been assigned a Belgian national registration number (Numéro National / Rijksregisternummer). The number itself MUST NOT appear in this file.

has_passport_BE.description: Whether the customer holds a Belgian passport. No passport number.

has_passport_other.description: Whether the customer holds a non-Belgian passport. No passport number.

validity_end_id_card.description: Month-bucket expiry of the Belgian-issued chip card (YYYY-MM), or null. Renamed from validity_end_eID per the has_eID → has_id_card rename (2026-05-18 fold).

validity_end_residence_card.description: Month-bucket expiry of the residence permit card (YYYY-MM), or null.

validity_end_work_permit.description: Month-bucket expiry of the work permit (YYYY-MM), or null.

validity_end_passport_BE.description: Month-bucket expiry of the Belgian passport (YYYY-MM), or null.

validity_end_passport_other.description: Month-bucket expiry of the non-Belgian passport (YYYY-MM), or null.

document_inventory.description: Boolean presence flags and YYYY-MM month-bucket expiry dates for documents the customer holds. No document numbers, no full expiry days.

active_procedures.description: Kebab-case skill IDs for all ongoing procedures. Cross-references into memory/procedure_progress_*.md files.

transitions_in_progress.description: Life transitions currently in progress. Extensible in future schema versions.

card_type.description: Belgian residence card or visa type. 23-value enum per the W25.15 card-type enum survey (2026-05-19): replaces the design-doc 16-value draft after Stream G identified 3 material defects. Removed: single_permit (annotation on A/B, not a card type — capture work-route in notes). Added: D (legacy long-term resident, archived format), I + J (intra-corporate transferee cards, live since Dec 2021), M_plus (Brexit-WA permanent variant), E + E_plus (legacy EU registration cards), EU + EU_plus (current EU registration cards, replaced E/E+ in May 2021). Label-correction: F is currently issued (not archived); B is currently issued (not archived). EU_citizen remains the value for EU/EEA/CH citizens present without registering.

start_month.description: Start of the card validity period, YYYY-MM month-bucket precision.

end_month.description: End of the card validity period (YYYY-MM), or the literal 'current' when the card is still held.

notes.description: Optional free-text note for this card entry, e.g. 'changed job at this point'. Max 200 chars.

belgian_residence_card_history.description: Chronological list of Belgian residence cards and visas held by the customer. Card type is an enum (23 values per W25.15 survey); dates are YYYY-MM month-bucket. No card numbers, no document identifiers. EU citizens may have a row with card_type=EU_citizen and no start/end dates. Added W25.12; enum synced to W25.15 survey 2026-05-19.
